---
title: "Lecture 07 Notes: Robot Modeling IV - Dynamics of Open Chains"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 07 slides](../slides/07-robot-modeling-3-dynamics.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Dynamics explains how torques produce acceleration and motion.
- Open-chain manipulators are commonly modeled with \(M, C, g\) terms.
- Accurate dynamic models improve simulation and model-based control.

## Standard manipulator equation

\[
M(q)\ddot{q} + C(q,\dot{q})\dot{q} + g(q) = \tau
\]

## Review questions

1. What role does the mass matrix \(M(q)\) play?
2. Why are Coriolis terms coupled with velocity?
3. When can friction and unmodeled effects dominate predictions?
